top_signals.title: Oil Prices Jump After U.S. Strikes Iran Over Downed Apache
top_signals.summary: Oil prices jumped in early Asian trading on Wednesday after the United States launched what it described as self-defense strikes against Iranian military targets near the Strait of Hormuz.  At the time of writing, Brent crude was up 1.03% at $92.39 per barrel, while West Texas Intermediate had gained 0.91% to trade at $89.00 per barrel. U.S. Central Command said the strikes were in response to Iran's downing of a U.S. Army Apache helicopter in the region. U.S. forces then "struck Iranian air defense, ground control stations, and surveillance…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Peru election too close to call as vote tally drags on
top_signals.summary: Investors weigh mining and economic risks as Peru’s presidential race remains deadlocked with most ballots counted.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Larvotto Resources inks gold offtake deal with Glencore
top_signals.summary: Deal is for the sale of gold concentrate for 7 years from its Hillgrove antimony-gold project in New South Wales.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Op-Ed: Lithium’s threat isn’t demand, but pricing
top_signals.summary: Battery substitutes are advancing, but the bigger challenge for producers is slower growth and tighter margins.
